description:
A deployment engineer has handed you a fresh copy of the next release located under
    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/  
    It already contains three micro-services—each in its own sub-directory:

        /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/auth-service
        /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/billing-service
        /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/report-service

    Every service directory is guaranteed to contain:

        1. manifest.yaml   – a small YAML file with the structure

                metadata:
                  service: <service-name>
                  version: "<semver>"
                  container:
                    port: <integer-port>

           The indentation, keys and quotation marks are exactly as above; only the values
           differ between services.

        2. config.env      – an .env-style file containing one KEY=VALUE pair per line.  
           No blank lines, no comments.

    Your job is to create a reproducible “deployment-bundle” under
        /home/user/deployment-output/
    consisting of three artefacts derived from the input files.  The automated grader will
    look for these files and verify their exact byte content, so follow the
    formatting rules precisely.

    1. /home/user/deployment-output/env_summary.log  
       • Pure ASCII text  
       • One line per service, sorted alphabetically by service name.  
       • Format of each line (no extra spaces):  
             <service>,<variable_count>
         where <variable_count> is the total number of KEY=VALUE lines found in that
         service’s config.env.

    2. /home/user/deployment-output/all_env.csv  
       • UTF-8 CSV with Unix line-feeds.  
       • First line (header) must be exactly:  
             service,key,value
       • One subsequent row per individual environment variable in **all** config.env
         files.  
       • Columns are:
             service  – service directory name  
             key      – the variable name exactly as it appears before the ‘=’  
             value    – everything after the first ‘=’ on the line (it can contain
                         symbols but no embedded newlines)  
       • Rows sorted first by service name, then by key, both ascending and case-sensitive.  
       • Do NOT surround fields with quotes unless the field would otherwise violate CSV
         syntax (none of the provided data will).

    3. /home/user/deployment-output/manifest_index.json  
       • UTF-8, pretty-printed JSON (two-space indentation, newline after each element).  
       • Top-level is an array whose elements are objects with exactly three keys in this
         order:
             "service", "version", "port"
       • Each object’s values must be extracted from the matching manifest.yaml.  
       • The array elements must be sorted alphabetically by the “service” value.

    Constraints & notes for the engineer (you):

       • All three artefacts must be created entirely by shell commands; feel free to use
         parallelisation tools (e.g. xargs -P, GNU parallel, background subshells, …)
         whenever it makes the workflow faster.  Verification checks only the results, not
         how you got there.

       • Do NOT modify any original files under /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/.

       • The directory /home/user/deployment-output/ is guaranteed to be writable by the
         current unprivileged user but may not exist yet—create it if necessary.

       • When you are satisfied, you can cat the generated files to the terminal so a human
         reviewer can glance at them, but remember the grader will read them directly from
         disk.

    Outcome expected by the grader:
       ✔ The directory /home/user/deployment-output/ exists.  
       ✔ The three files listed above exist and have content that matches the specification
         exactly (byte-for-byte comparison).

    Accomplish the task using any set of standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in
    a minimal Debian-like environment.
truth:
Initial file-system state that is already present before the agent starts:

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/auth-service/manifest.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------------------
    metadata:
      service: auth-service
      version: "2.1.0"
      container:
        port: 7000

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/auth-service/config.env
    ------------------------------------------------------
    DB_HOST=auth-db.internal
    DB_PORT=5432
    JWT_SECRET=s3cr3t
    LOG_LEVEL=INFO

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/billing-service/manifest.yaml
    ------------------------------------------------------------
    metadata:
      service: billing-service
      version: "3.4.2"
      container:
        port: 7100

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/billing-service/config.env
    ---------------------------------------------------------
    DB_HOST=billing-db.internal
    DB_PORT=5432
    PAYMENT_GATEWAY_TOKEN=abcd1234
    LOG_LEVEL=INFO
    CURRENCY=USD

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/report-service/manifest.yaml
    -----------------------------------------------------------
    metadata:
      service: report-service
      version: "1.8.0"
      container:
        port: 7200

    /home/user/releases/2024-06-01/report-service/config.env
    --------------------------------------------------------
    DB_HOST=report-db.internal
    DB_PORT=5432
    REPORT_CACHE_SIZE=512
    LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG


    Exact contents expected **after** successful task execution:

    /home/user/deployment-output/env_summary.log
    -------------------------------------------
    auth-service,4
    billing-service,5
    report-service,4

    /home/user/deployment-output/all_env.csv
    ---------------------------------------
    service,key,value
    auth-service,DB_HOST,auth-db.internal
    auth-service,DB_PORT,5432
    auth-service,JWT_SECRET,s3cr3t
    auth-service,LOG_LEVEL,INFO
    billing-service,CURRENCY,USD
    billing-service,DB_HOST,billing-db.internal
    billing-service,DB_PORT,5432
    billing-service,LOG_LEVEL,INFO
    billing-service,PAYMENT_GATEWAY_TOKEN,abcd1234
    report-service,DB_HOST,report-db.internal
    report-service,DB_PORT,5432
    report-service,LOG_LEVEL,DEBUG
    report-service,REPORT_CACHE_SIZE,512

    /home/user/deployment-output/manifest_index.json
    ------------------------------------------------
    [
      {
        "service": "auth-service",
        "version": "2.1.0",
        "port": 7000
      },
      {
        "service": "billing-service",
        "version": "3.4.2",
        "port": 7100
      },
      {
        "service": "report-service",
        "version": "1.8.0",
        "port": 7200
      }
    ]
